    About this item

    • 【Ultra-low Latency&Free and Optional Remote Access】High-definition 1080p video at 60 FPS with 30-60 millisecond latency, using efficient H.264 encoding. Smooth mouse and keyboard action transfer for responsive remote interaction.Remote management via JetKVM Cloud using WebRTC. Secure and a fast direct connection, even behind the most restrictive NAT environments, with our STUN and TURN servers.
    • 【Open Source&Interactively Upgradeable System】Written in Golang, running on Linux, making it easy to modify the software on your JetKVM device. Want to customize the JetKVM? Simply patch the software and upload it to the JetKVM device through SSH, and you’re good to go.Continuous interaction allows for ongoing optimization and upgrades of the product system. Regular updates provide further improvements.
    • 【Multi-System Flexible Internet Access】Remote access is enabled through our web-based cloud control, supporting Windows, macOS, and Linux systems without any software installation. Three connectivity modes are supported: local access, remote access, and LAN Wake-on-LAN to power on or wake the device.
    • 【Super Secure Encryption Methods】Local Authentication Mode: Optionally password-protect device access to the local network. Remote Access Password Protection: Access tokens are stored as HTTP-only cookies in your browser for 7 days, enabling effortless password protection. Remote access requires OIDC authentication, meaning all access controls are processed locally on the device.No personal information is collected by JETKVM, ensuring a private experience.
    • 【Unlimited Hackability】The hardware is fully customizable. Through the RJ11 extension port, extra hardware capabilities can easily be added by anyone. It still has been used like a temperature sensor.As optional add-ons, the hardware extensions using the extension port, enabling features like ATX Power Control, Serial Console and AD/DC power control on your device.
    • 【ATX Power Control & DC Power Control】A control board for managing computer power status. It also includes a USB-C port for connecting a 5V power supply. JetKVM can then be powered via an RJ11 cable. 5V power can also be supplied via pin connectors. PCIe slot covers allow for neat cable management within the chassis. The DC power control expansion board supports power management for connected DC-powered devices (such as mini PCs or NAS devices), supporting 12 to 20 volt DC input.
    • 【Expanding power supplies are available for sale】ATX expansion boards and DC power control boards are also available in our store. Please check our product listings.Please be aware when purchasing products from the BitPC store.The products sold in other shop are not manufactured by BitPC.

    Product description

    JetKVM-Efficient remote management of computers

    JetKVM is a highly efficient open-source IP KVM switch. It provides a remote control solution for target computers using just one set of peripherals (keyboard,mouse,monitor). Efficiently remote management of computers, servers, and workstations,such as operating systems, or adjusting BIOS settings.

    Seamless Remote Control

    Main features

    Seamless Remote Control

    • Experience fluid control and crystal-clear video quality that makes remote access feel local. Perfect for IT professionals, developers, and power users who demand responsive remote management.

    Ultra-low Latency

    • 1080p@60FPS video with 30-60ms latency using H.264 encoding. Smooth mouse and keyboard interaction for responsive remote control.

    Free & Optional Remote Access

    • Remote management via JetKVM Cloud using WebRTC.

    Open-source Software

    • Written in Golang on Linux. Easily customizable through SSH access to the JetKVM device.

    Important Notice about video

    • Desktop PCs have iGPU (integrated) and dGPU (discrete) graphics. Their output ports are shown below.For iGPU only: Connect to motherboard ports.For dGPU installed: Connect to graphics card ports.
    • Warning: Connecting to iGPU ports with a dGPU will cause no display / black screen.

    Important Power Notice

    • Use a 5V power source only, supplied via the controlled computer’s USB-A or USB-C port. USB-C to USB-A Cable power supply is recommended.
    • Y-cable splitter is not recommended: Standard USB PD (Power Delivery) chargers are incompatible with the Y-cable. Most PD chargers will fail to provide stable output without negotiation lines.

    No response on first use?Unplug and reconnect all cables securely, then wait for automatic adaptation.

    Super Secure Encryption Methods

    Secure Connectivity for JetKVM

    Built-in VPN protects private workflows

    • JetKVM KVM over IP Cloud (WebRTC/STUN/TURN) bypasses restrictive NAT for encrypted direct connections. 100% free cloud access, ideal for remote workers/travelers.

    JETKVM's security modes

    • Local Mode：Local mode allows you to set a login password within a local area.
    • Remote Access Password Protection: Access tokens are stored as HTTP-only cookies in your browser for 7 days,Regular re-verification is required to ensure the absolute safety of the equipment.
    • OIDC authentication：Remote access requires OIDC authentication, meaning all access controls are processed locally on the device.

    Three Ways of Networking

    Local Access

    To access the JetKVM web UI, you'll need the device's IP address, which is displayed on the front screen of the JetKVM device along with the MAC address. Simply refer to this screen, note the IP address, and use it to navigate to the web UI in your browser.

    Remote Access

    Remote Access allows you to connect to your JetKVM device from anywhere on the internet. Through our cloud dashboard, you can log in, get an overview and control any of your cloud-enabled devices. This means you can access and manage your JetKVM devices from your phone or computer, anywhere in the world with an internet connection.

    Wake on Lan

    Wake on LAN is a standard feature that allows a computer to be powered on or awakened from sleep mode by receiving a network message, known as a "magic packet." This message is sent from one device to another on the local area network (LAN).

    Please note, the target computer must be configured to listen for incoming magic packets to be awakened.

    Open-Source Go/React WebRTC Dashboard with Lightweight Linux System

    Special Features and Power Options

    Special Features of JetKVM

    • After installing the driver, JetKVM remains accessible during BIOS or UEFI boot processes. This proves extremely useful for tasks like reinstalling operating systems or mounting ISO images to install applications on the target host.
    • Beyond standard keyboard and mouse support, JetKVM includes a virtual keyboard feature, providing basic keyboard input via the UI. This enables sending individual keystrokes to the remote computer as needed.
    • Note: Currently, JetKVM supports only US keyboard layouts, with host key mappings defaulting to US settings.
    • The rear of JetKVM features an RJ-12 expansion port designed for complete customization and flexibility. Whether integrating additional hardware functions or providing custom add-ons, the expansion port offers limitless possibilities for extension.

    Power Options

    • Primary Power Method:The most common way to power the JetKVM is through its USB-C port, connected directly to the computer you're controlling. Many computers have USB ports with constant power, though in some cases, you may need to enable it in the BIOS or UEFI settings (often labeled as "USB Power During Standby" or "USB Always On"), so be sure to check there if it's not working by default.
    • Alternative Power Options:USB-C Power/Data Splitter (Included),DC Extension,ATX Board Extension.

    Three Expansion Modules

    ATX Power Extension

    A control board that manages computer power states through the JetKVM's RJ-12 extension port. Connect to your motherboard's front panel headers to gain remote power control over your PC.

    Each pin features dual headers: one set for KVM control and another for the original front panel buttons -- meaning you don't have to choose between them. Both can be connected simultaneously.

    Comes with a PCIe slot cover bracket for clean cable routing through your PC case without requiring an actual PCIe slot.

    Remote Power Control for any DC device

    Add remote power cycling to your JetKVM setup. Plug into the RJ-12 port, route your device's DC power through the module, and control power directly from the browser with the JetKVM.

    It supports DC input ranging from 12 to 20 volts, and we're including adapters to support 5.5x2.1 mm connections as well.

    The extension can also power the JetKVM itself through the RJ-12 connection, streamlining setup and reducing the need for additional power sources.

    Serial Console Extension for JetKVM

    Extend your JetKVM capabilities with serial console access. This extension provides both RS-232 and RJ-12 connectivity options, allowing you to access serial consoles directly from your browser through the JetKVM interface.

    Connect to devices with either RS-232 serial ports commonly found on network equipment, servers, and embedded systems.

    The extension integrates seamlessly with your JetKVM setup, providing reliable serial communication for configuration, troubleshooting, and remote management tasks.

    Warranty & Support

    Amazon.com Return Policy:You may return any new computer purchased from Amazon.com that is "dead on arrival," arrives in damaged condition, or is still in unopened boxes, for a full refund within 30 days of purchase. Amazon.com reserves the right to test "dead on arrival" returns and impose a customer fee equal to 15 percent of the product sales price if the customer misrepresents the condition of the product. Any returned computer that is damaged through customer misuse, is missing parts, or is in unsellable condition due to customer tampering will result in the customer being charged a higher restocking fee based on the condition of the product. Amazon.com will not accept returns of any desktop or notebook computer more than 30 days after you receive the shipment. New, used, and refurbished products purchased from Marketplace vendors are subject to the returns policy of the individual vendor.

    How customer reviews and ratings work

    Customer Reviews, including Product Star Ratings help customers to learn more about the product and decide whether it is the right product for them.

    To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyzed reviews to verify trustworthiness.

    Amazon Customer
    5 out of 5 stars
    It Works Once You Get It Connected Correctly
    I found out about this lil gem from a YouTube channel. I think this is one of the better ideas man could have come up with. I was going to order this from the 2 different manufacturers that have them. However, Amazon had the rewards that applied to the purchase price as well as free 2 day shipping so I went that route. Let me say this so you can prepare yourself for what's in store. Although this device comes with most of the cables(HDMI, USB-C to USB-A), you will still need to purchase a network cable(RJ-45) and another USB-C to USB-A cable. You see, there's a USB-C SPLITTER cable that comes with the device but you need to have both of those inputs occupied as one of them powers the device and the other is for data. The icons are on imprinted on the housing of the cable. Once you get all of that connected, there's a few other steps to take. From the computer you connected the device to, go to the IP address that is displayed on the front of the device and proceed through the steps to create an account. You can log into the account using your existing GOOGLE login info. Once completed, login and go to the SETTINGS icon(on the upper right side). On the right side, scroll down to the ACCESS tab and make certain you ENABLE remote access by setting up the CLOUD security function. After this step, go back to the desktop and right click on any blank area and select DISPLAY SETTINGS. In the section where you can see the picture of a monitor, look for the area where you can change the monitor displays(Duplicate these displays, Extend These Displays, Show Only on 1, Show Only on 2). Choose DUPLICATE THESE DISPLAYS and click on SAVE or APPLY. From there, you should be able to fully have control of the device. There may be a delay(depending) in seeing the screen from an outside login but just be patient and it will come into play.

    • Amazon Customer
      5.0 out of 5 stars Service, Quality & Reliability
      Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
      Verified Purchase
      JetKVM has been an excellent device and easily one of the best KVM solutions I’ve used. The setup is straightforward, the interface is intuitive, and overall performance is rock solid. One of the biggest highlights is the low latency interactions feel fast and responsive, making remote access genuinely smooth rather than frustrating.

      The reliability and video quality really set it apart from many other KVM devices on the market. Everything works exactly as expected, which is honestly what you want most from a tool like this.
      The only thing that could make JetKVM absolutely perfect would be a built-in voice/audio option. If voice support were included, it would hands down be the best KVM device available, no contest.
      Highly recommended for anyone looking for a fast, reliable, and well-designed KVM solution.
      Read more
      One person found this helpful
      Helpful
       Report
    • A Power
      5.0 out of 5 stars WAY better than I expected.
      Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
      Verified Purchase
      This just works out of the box. No phone app necessary, no cloud account necessary. I plugged in the HDMI, USB, and network and the screen showed its IP. I went to that IP in my web browser and it just works. The speed and quality are more than I was expecting. I noticed a few reviews that mentioned the usb splitter does not work, this is not true. There is a usb icon on one and the lightning bolt/power icon on the other. I used it both with and without external 5v usb power. Just make sure to pay attention when connecting external power to use the correct connections. I was very close to purchasing a more popular brand but saw this at the last moment and I am very happy with that choice. It is also extremely fast to move it to a different device when necessary. It only takes a few seconds for it to power on and get its IP. It was extremely well packaged in a hard cardboard box similar to new phone boxes. The unit is metal, not plastic. This really does seem to be a built well.
      Read more
      4 people found this helpful
      Helpful
       Report
    • Gmoney
      5.0 out of 5 stars It Works Once You Get It Connected Correctly
      Reviewed in the United States on March 19, 2026
      Verified Purchase
      I found out about this lil gem from a YouTube channel. I think this is one of the better ideas man could have come up with. I was going to order this from the 2 different manufacturers that have them. However, Amazon had the rewards that applied to the purchase price as well as free 2 day shipping so I went that route.
      Let me say this so you can prepare yourself for what's in store. Although this device comes with most of the cables(HDMI, USB-C to USB-A), you will still need to purchase a network cable(RJ-45) and another USB-C to USB-A cable. You see, there's a USB-C SPLITTER cable that comes with the device but you need to have both of those inputs occupied as one of them powers the device and the other is for data. The icons are on imprinted on the housing of the cable. Once you get all of that connected, there's a few other steps to take. From the computer you connected the device to, go to the IP address that is displayed on the front of the device and proceed through the steps to create an account. You can log into the account using your existing GOOGLE login info. Once completed, login and go to the SETTINGS icon(on the upper right side). On the right side, scroll down to the ACCESS tab and make certain you ENABLE remote access by setting up the CLOUD security function. After this step, go back to the desktop and right click on any blank area and select DISPLAY SETTINGS. In the section where you can see the picture of a monitor, look for the area where you can change the monitor displays(Duplicate these displays, Extend These Displays, Show Only on 1, Show Only on 2). Choose DUPLICATE THESE DISPLAYS and click on SAVE or APPLY. From there, you should be able to fully have control of the device. There may be a delay(depending) in seeing the screen from an outside login but just be patient and it will come into play.
      Read more
      Gmoney
      5.0 out of 5 stars
      It Works Once You Get It Connected Correctly

      Reviewed in the United States on March 19, 2026
      I found out about this lil gem from a YouTube channel. I think this is one of the better ideas man could have come up with. I was going to order this from the 2 different manufacturers that have them. However, Amazon had the rewards that applied to the purchase price as well as free 2 day shipping so I went that route.
      Let me say this so you can prepare yourself for what's in store. Although this device comes with most of the cables(HDMI, USB-C to USB-A), you will still need to purchase a network cable(RJ-45) and another USB-C to USB-A cable. You see, there's a USB-C SPLITTER cable that comes with the device but you need to have both of those inputs occupied as one of them powers the device and the other is for data. The icons are on imprinted on the housing of the cable. Once you get all of that connected, there's a few other steps to take. From the computer you connected the device to, go to the IP address that is displayed on the front of the device and proceed through the steps to create an account. You can log into the account using your existing GOOGLE login info. Once completed, login and go to the SETTINGS icon(on the upper right side). On the right side, scroll down to the ACCESS tab and make certain you ENABLE remote access by setting up the CLOUD security function. After this step, go back to the desktop and right click on any blank area and select DISPLAY SETTINGS. In the section where you can see the picture of a monitor, look for the area where you can change the monitor displays(Duplicate these displays, Extend These Displays, Show Only on 1, Show Only on 2). Choose DUPLICATE THESE DISPLAYS and click on SAVE or APPLY. From there, you should be able to fully have control of the device. There may be a delay(depending) in seeing the screen from an outside login but just be patient and it will come into play.

    • TJ
      3.0 out of 5 stars Mostly good
      Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
      Verified Purchase
      Overall a decent product. Easy setup. It seems to be well built. The software is easy to use. It's a little expensive for what it is, but the price isn't completely out of line. I purchased two of these, and with both of them, every 2nd or 3rd time I connect, the video will not load. I get stuck with a spinning circle and 'loading video feed.' I restart the KVM, and I can then connect without a problem. It's a nuisance not a deal breaker, but I hope this issue gets resolved sooner rather than later.

    • Todd Tran
      4.0 out of 5 stars I can't believe I hadn't found this before! If you're here, just buy it already.
      Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
      Verified Purchase
      Let me explain my title.
      I don't doubt this will work flawlessly on a Windows, Linux, Mac, etc., system. I have not tried. My application is to put a remote head on an Amcrest NVR which really needs it's own monitor, keyboard and mouse. It has its own webUI but it's not the same as the console UI. There are things you can configure in the console UI that you can't do in the webUI. This little gem saved the day.

      But, let's backup. I typically don't like to spend lots of money on things that you "set it and forget it, so I was looking at either this unit or the IP_KVM (around $70) and the JetKVM ($100). I decided to spend a little more for this product that looked more... DONE, and I am happy I did.

      My first impression when I opened the box was, "This thing is a BRICK!". It has some heft to it. Seriously, it's more solid and heavier than it looks. As opposed to the IP-KVM, which looks like it's a cobbled breakout assembly in a 3D printed box. That's my blind opinion, since I did not purchase the IP_KVM product.

      Hooking it up is pretty much a no-brainer. It really is, "if the connector fit, that's where it goes". The only 2 things that could confuse you is the USB-C splitter. However, one head has a thunderbolt on it, meaning the POWER plug. You'll need a user-provided USB-A to USB-C cable to connect to power. And the other head uses the included (short) USB cable that connects to the "target device" for keyboard/mouse emulation.

      Once it was all connected, I accidentally swiped the display with a finger and realized it has a tough screen, albiet, it doesn't do much but shows device status, but that's a nice surprise.

      I would give 5 stars for this product, however, I was under the impression this device is accessible via VNC, but it can only be accessed via webUI. That's ok. You just have to remember it's another device that is accessible by web browser.
      Read more
      3 people found this helpful
      Helpful
